title: Closure version binding for hot code loading
purpose: Closures (funs) are heap-allocated objects that capture a module name, a lambda index into the module's lambdas table, free variables, and arity. Under hot code loading, if the module is reloaded with a different lambda table, the closure's function_index becomes stale — it references the wrong lambda or goes out of bounds. Unlike call_ext targets (which can be resolved dynamically by MFA), closures encode a positional index that has no meaning outside its original module version.
task: Implement unique fun identifiers for closures so that closures created in one module version can be correctly resolved against a different version of the same module. Update the closure heap layout, GC traversal, and dispatch logic.
boundaries: SHALL NOT change the heap layout of closures unless necessary (adding generation and unique_id fields is acceptable)
SHALL handle closures created in old module versions that are called after the module is reloaded
SHALL ensure closures work correctly in the trampoline pattern (higher-order BIFs like lists:map that call closures via re-entry)

requirements.title: Unique fun identifier
requirements.spec: WHEN a module is loaded, THE SYSTEM SHALL compute a unique identifier for each lambda entry from a hash of (module_name, function_name, arity, num_free) so that closures can be matched against the correct lambda regardless of table position.
acceptance: LambdaEntry gains a unique_id: u64 field, computed from a hash of (module_name, function_name, arity, num_free) at load time
Module.lambdas entries include the unique_id
The unique_id is deterministic for the same source function across recompilations (same function name + arity = same unique_id)
New method: Module::find_lambda_by_id(unique_id: u64) scans lambdas for matching unique_id
Test: create two versions of a module with the same functions in different order. Verify find_lambda_by_id returns the correct lambda in both versions.

Note: if a module has two closures with the same generated name, arity, and num_free (unlikely in Gleam but possible in Erlang), they would collide. This is a documented limitation for the Gleam use case.

requirements.title: Closure heap layout gains generation and unique_id
requirements.spec: WHEN a closure is created, THE SYSTEM SHALL store the module generation and lambda unique_id in the closure's heap representation so that version mismatches can be detected and resolved at call time.
acceptance: Closure heap layout becomes: [header] [module_atom] [function_index] [arity] [num_free] [generation] [unique_id] [free_var_0] ... [free_var_n]
write_closure takes additional generation: u64 and unique_id: u64 parameters
Closure accessor gains: generation() and unique_id() methods
Header tag or word count updated to accommodate the two extra words
All existing closure tests updated for new layout
make_fun2/make_fun3 opcode handlers pass the correct generation (from the currently executing module) and unique_id (from the lambda entry)

requirements.title: Version-aware closure dispatch
requirements.spec: WHEN calling a closure whose generation does not match the current module's generation, THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve the lambda by unique_id instead of by positional index.
acceptance: call_fun checks if closure.generation() matches the target module's generation
If generations match: use closure.function_index() directly (fast path, O(1))
If generations differ: use module.find_lambda_by_id(closure.unique_id()) to find the lambda in the current version
If the lambda is not found in the current version (function was removed): check the registry's old version. If found there, execute against the old version. If not found anywhere, raise badfun error.

Test: create closure in module A v1. Reload module A to v2 (same functions, different order). Call closure — resolves via unique_id. Reload module A to v3 (function removed). Call closure — raises badfun.

requirements.title: Trampoline compatibility
requirements.spec: WHEN a closure is called via the trampoline pattern (TrampolineRequest::CallClosure), THE SYSTEM SHALL use the same version-aware dispatch as R3.
acceptance: Audit TrampolineRequest::CallClosure handling in trampoline.rs
Ensure the trampoline passes the closure through the same version-aware dispatch as R3

Test: create a closure in module A v1. Pass it to lists:map (trampoline path). Mid-map, reload module A to v2. The closure continues executing against the version it was created with (v1 generation + unique_id resolution).

requirements.title: GC traversal updated for new closure layout
requirements.spec: WHEN the garbage collector traces a closure, THE SYSTEM SHALL use the updated free variable offset that accounts for the new generation and unique_id words.
acceptance: rewrite_copied_object in gc/mod.rs correctly identifies the new free variable start offset for closures
Generation and unique_id words are copied verbatim (they are not heap pointers, no tracing needed)
Free variables at the new offset are still correctly traced and rewritten during collection
Test: create a closure with free variables. Trigger GC. Verify closure is intact with correct generation, unique_id, and free variables after collection.

verification: cargo test --workspace passes with all existing tests (closure tests updated for new layout)
Closures created in module v1 resolve correctly after module is reloaded to v2 with different lambda order
GC correctly traces closures with the new heap layout
Trampoline path handles version-aware closure dispatch
Closure calling a removed function raises badfun
